content.full_text:
December 5, 1986

Mr. Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
The International Building
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-305

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

In your letter of October 27, 1986, you requested written
confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on October 22,
1986, by Kim Taylor of the Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division.

QUESTION

May filers of the second pre-election campaign statement due
before the November 4, 1986, general election use an alternative
overnight delivery service (e.g., Federal Express) rather than
guaranteed overnight delivery service through the U.S. Postal
Service or personal delivery?

CONCLUSION

The Commission has advised filers that they may use an
alternative guaranteed overnight delivery service in lieu of the
U.S. Postal Service and still be in compliance with the provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

ANALYSIS

Government Code Sections 84200.7(a)(2) and (b)(2), 84200.8(b)
and (C), and 84202.5(a) provide that the pre-election statements
required by those sections shall be "delivered in person or by
guaranteed overnight mail through the United States Postal
Service."

_______________________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Mr. Bagatelos
Page Two


     The Commission has found that the U.S. Postal Service does not
guarantee overnight delivery service to every city in California.
In addition, the Commission has received complaints from filers
that their campaign disclosure statements were not delivered
overnight as guaranteed by the U.S. Postal Service.

     The Political Reform Act has been amended to provide that,
effective January 1, 1987, statements required to be filed
pursuant to the sections cited above may be filed by any
guaranteed overnight delivery service or by personal delivery.

     For these reasons, the Commission has advised filers that they
may use any guaranteed overnight delivery service in lieu of the
U.S. Postal Service.

     Please contact me if I may be of further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                   By   Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:kt

LAW OFFICES OF
BAGATELOS & FADEM
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

BARRY FADEM
PETER A. BAGATELOS

THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING
801 CALIFORNIA STREET
SUITE 1801
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108

FPPC

Nov 4 3 37 PM '86
TELEPHONE
(415) 982-7100

October 27, 1986


Ms. Jeannie Prichard
Fair Political Practices
Technical Assistance Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 94814

Re: Use of Overnight Delivery for Campaign Reports

Dear Jeannie:

     On October 22, 1986, I spoke with Kim of your office
regarding the manner of delivery of the twelve day pre-election
statements, as required by the Political Reform Act. The
current requirement in the law is that such statements must be
sent by guaranteed overnight mail through the U.S. Postal
Service or delivered in person no later than midnight on the
filing deadline date.

     Kim advised me that your office has been advising
filers that they may use alternative overnight delivery
services, such as Federal Express, in lieu of the U.S. Postal
Service guaranteed overnight program. Use of other overnight
delivery services would still constitute compliance with the
Political Reform Act requirements that such reports must be sent
by guaranteed overnight mail, as long as they are deposited with
the overnight carrier no later than midnight on the filing
deadline date.

     We would like to receive written confirmation of this
advice so that we may rely on it in the future. Please advise
if you require additional information.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]

                                        Peter A. Bagatelos

PAB:asp
